name: Spine
description: The spine of the scapula is a prominent ridge on the posterior surface and is triangular in shape.
It separates the posterior surface into 2 fossae.
The supraspinous fossa above and the infraspinous fossa below.
It broadens laterally as the acromion process.
It provides proximal attachment for part of the deltoid muscle.
The uppermost fibers of the trapezius muscle partially insert on the spine.
